A1_m_equals_y2_reproduces_koide.premise: m_a = y_a^2 (pair condensate bilinear in constituent amplitude); y_a = T1u vector component
A1_m_equals_y2_reproduces_koide.consequence: sqrt(m) is the cubic vector, Koide is about y

A2_power_one_half_is_special.reading: only the bilinear variable m=y^2 (s=1/2) hits the clean rational -> supports the Cooper-pair origin of sqrt(m)

B1_two_thirds_is_range_midpoint.reading: Q=2/3 is the exact midpoint between the fully-democratic floor (1/3) and the single-axis ceiling (1); leptons sit at maximal democratic<->hierarchical balance

B3_symmetric_gap_is_degenerate.verdict: a flavour-blind (O_h-symmetric) gap drives all three to a common value (degenerate, Q->1/3); it CANNOT produce the observed hierarchy or Q=2/3 -> equipartition is a large explicit symmetry-breaking, NOT supplied by symmetric dynamics. sqrt(2) is therefore NOT derived here.

B4_C3v_forces_two_fold_degeneracy.reading: the cube's natural body-diagonal site symmetry (C3v) hits Q=2/3 only at nu/mu=1/sqrt2 but with two masses EQUAL; the 3 distinct leptons need the lower (orthorhombic) symmetry of F76-C1, where sqrt2 is no longer fixed by symmetry

_summary.derived: m = y^2 (sqrt m is the T1u amplitude) GIVEN the Cooper-pair bilinear premise; data pick s=1/2 (A2)
_summary.NOT_derived: the value sqrt(2) (Q=2/3): symmetric cube dynamics give Q=1/3 (degenerate, B3) and C3v forces [2,1] (B4); equipartition is a critical/maximally-broken condition the symmetric model does not supply
